title: Fixing CSS Pseudo-Element Styles with Inconsistent Box Model

statement.markdown: You're given a CSS selector string and a list of rules. Your task is to parse the CSS selector and apply the rules to generate the final CSS styles. The styles should be in the following format: `selector { property: value; }`. The rules are in the format of a dictionary where the key is the property name and the value is the property value. However, there's a bug in the code that causes the box model properties (border, padding, margin, width, height) to be applied inconsistently. Your task is to fix the bug and make sure the box model properties are applied correctly.
statement.inputFormat: A CSS selector string and a list of rules in a dictionary format
statement.outputFormat: A string representing the final CSS styles in the format `selector { property: value; }`
statement.notes: The CSS selector can contain pseudo-elements and the rules dictionary can contain multiple properties.

examples.explanation: The box model properties (border, padding) are applied consistently
The box model properties (margin, width) are applied consistently

hints.text: Check the box model properties in the rules dictionary
